behaviors.description: An image containing explicit content is flagged and held for review, not published.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is the vision model’s real classification quality plus downstream hold/publish gating. MOCK-MISS: a unit test of decision logic misses misclassification. REAL-INFRA: no OS/subprocess requirement is stated. LLM-DEP: yes, correctness depends on real model judgment of explicitness. MULTI-STEP: single agentic decision with deterministic routing, so Workflow is not required. Rejection gate: rejected Workflow because behavior text specifies one moderation decision path, not multiple agentic stages.
behaviors.test_description: Run real image samples (including explicit content) through the production moderation path with the real vision model enabled; assert explicit samples are marked flagged and persisted in held-for-review state, and are not published.

behaviors.description: A text post containing hate speech is flagged regardless of obfuscation techniques (e.g., letter substitution, unicode lookalikes).
behaviors.justification: This is directly about model robustness to adversarial text variants. MOCK-MISS/MOCK-HIDE: stubs always pass and cannot reveal failures on obfuscation. REAL-INFRA: no specific OS/subprocess dependency required. LLM-DEP: yes, model output quality is the core behavior. Rejection gate: rejected Workflow because this behavior targets one model decision per post, not a chained multi-agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Submit a corpus of obfuscated hate-speech variants to the real text moderation model through service APIs and assert each is flagged and held; include repeated runs to detect drift-sensitive misses.

behaviors.description: A post that passes both content checks is published rather than held or silently dropped.
behaviors.justification: Primary failure is module handoff/orchestration: image result + text result -> publish state transition. MOCK-MISS: yes, this can fail at service/repo/queue boundaries even if each function is correct. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network requirement in behavior text. LLM-DEP: disqualified by text 'passes both content checks' (inputs to decision are already pass outcomes), so model semantic quality is not under test. MULTI-STEP agentic not required.
behaviors.test_description: Wire moderation service, persistence, and publish path with deterministic pass results from classifier adapters; assert final state is published and verify no hold record or drop path is taken.

behaviors.description: The full moderation pipeline — image check, text check, publish decision — completes within 5 seconds of submission.
behaviors.justification: This is an end-to-end latency SLO over sequential agentic stages (vision + text + decision). REAL-INFRA: timing realism requires real service/runtime/network characteristics. LLM-DEP: execution includes real model calls whose latency/behavior matters. MULTI-STEP: yes, multiple agentic steps in sequence, so Workflow is the minimum boundary. Rejection gate: rejected System/Agentic because single-step tests cannot expose compounded pipeline latency and cross-stage queueing effects.
behaviors.test_description: Run end-to-end submissions through production-like deployment with real model calls for both image and text checks; measure p95/p99 completion time from ingest to final decision and assert <=5s threshold under peak-like concurrent load.

behaviors.description: When the AI vision model is unavailable, image posts are queued for retry rather than auto-approved.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is fallback orchestration on dependency failure (classifier adapter -> queueing -> decision policy). MOCK-MISS: cross-module state handoff can fail. LLM-DEP: no, this tests unavailability handling, not output quality. REAL-INFRA: could be System for transport-specific outages, but behavior text only requires handling 'unavailable' condition, which deterministic adapter failures can reproduce. Rejection gate: rejected Agentic because no model-content judgment is needed; rejected System because no explicit OS/network-specific failure mode is required.
behaviors.test_description: Use a failing vision-model adapter (timeout/unavailable error contract) in a wired service+queue integration test; assert image post is enqueued with retry metadata and explicitly not marked approved/published.

behaviors.description: The moderation decision for a borderline image is consistent when the same image is submitted multiple times within a short window.
behaviors.justification: Consistency is evaluated across repeated submissions, i.e., multiple agentic decisions over time. LLM-DEP: yes, borderline cases are sensitive to real model variance. MULTI-STEP: yes, sequence of submissions/decisions is intrinsic to behavior, making Workflow the true boundary. MOCK-HIDE: deterministic stubs cannot surface nondeterministic flips. Rejection gate: rejected Agentic because a single-call test cannot verify cross-call consistency window behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Submit the same borderline image repeatedly within the target window through the full moderation pipeline with real vision model calls and production consistency controls (e.g., cache/memoization if implemented); assert stable final moderation outcome across runs.
